reviewFocus: Check whether the packet preserves a trust-safe blocker clarification boundary instead of pretending the dispute evidence already proves one side correct.
Flag copied-truth risk, missing clarification questions, overconfident blocker language, and any drift from advisory clarification into approval, routing, launch, or product-decision authority.
Check that the packet keeps source handles opaque, uses only bounded summaries, and avoids leaking private implementation detail through blocker labels, evidence notes, or stakeholder shorthand.
Prefer reversible clarification work such as narrower questions, explicit caveats, and evidence-scope checks over irreversible product or execution recommendations.
Keep source resolution, domain interpretation, prioritization, approval, escalation, and any real-world action caller-owned.
outOfScope: Do not decide which side of the dispute is correct or whether the underlying blocker is real.
Do not infer product roadmaps, repository state, company roles, customer identities, or private implementation details from the opaque handles.
Do not approve launch, assign work, mutate tracker state, or recommend external communications.
